Answer: Supervised learning uses labeled data, while unsupervised learning uses unlabeled data.
Overfitting is when a model memorizes training data instead of generalizing. Prevented by cross-validation, regularization, or pruning.
Bias-variance tradeoff balances underfitting (high bias) and overfitting (high variance).
Cross-validation types: k-fold, stratified k-fold, leave-one-out, time-series split.
Precision = TP / (TP+FP), Recall = TP / (TP+FN), F1 = harmonic mean of precision & recall.
Confusion matrix shows TP, FP, TN, FN values for classification evaluation.
Classification predicts categories, regression predicts continuous values.
Dimensionality reduction reduces features while preserving important information.
PCA reduces dimensions using eigenvectors of covariance matrix.
Feature engineering is creating new features or modifying existing ones to improve model performance.
One-hot encoding converts categorical variables into binary vectors.
Bagging builds models independently; boosting builds models sequentially.
Random Forest is an ensemble of decision trees trained with bagging.
Gradient Boosting builds models sequentially, minimizing errors step by step.
XGBoost is an efficient, scalable gradient boosting library, popular for competitions.
Naive Bayes applies Bayes theorem assuming independence between features.
Outliers are extreme values; handled using capping, removal, or transformations.
Multicollinearity is high correlation among predictors; reduces model interpretability.
Correlation is association; causation is when one variable affects another.
Regularization adds penalty to model weights (L1 = Lasso, L2 = Ridge).
Ridge shrinks coefficients, Lasso can shrink some to zero (feature selection).
Logistic regression is used for binary classification problems.
ROC curve plots TPR vs FPR at different thresholds.
AUC score measures area under ROC; higher is better.
K-means partitions data into k clusters using distance-based minimization.
Hierarchical clustering builds a tree of clusters (agglomerative/divisive).
DBSCAN is a density-based clustering algorithm.
Recommender systems suggest items to users based on preferences.
Collaborative filtering uses user-item interactions; content-based uses item features.
Time series forecasting predicts future values using past data.
ARIMA combines autoregression, differencing, and moving average.
Stationarity means mean/variance are constant over time.
Moving average smooths time series by averaging values over a window.
Deep learning uses multi-layered neural networks to learn representations.
CNNs work well for images, RNNs for sequential data like text/speech.
Backpropagation updates weights using gradient descent.
Vanishing gradient occurs when gradients become very small in deep networks.
Dropout randomly disables neurons during training to reduce overfitting.
Batch GD uses all data; SGD uses one sample at a time.
Transfer learning uses pre-trained models for new tasks.
Reinforcement learning trains agents via rewards and penalties.
Q-learning is a value-based RL algorithm using action-value functions.
NLP processes and analyzes human language data.
Word2vec creates vector embeddings of words using neural networks.
TF-IDF measures importance of words based on frequency and rarity.
Embeddings represent words/sentences in vector space.
LDA is a topic modeling method assigning documents to topics.
Sentiment analysis detects opinions/emotions from text.
Big Data refers to large, complex datasets requiring advanced processing.
Data science is broad (insights + ML), data analytics focuses on analysis, ML builds predictive models.
